meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit: Master Portfolio Building for Students
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no portfolio' trap. Learn to build a high-impact SaaS starter kit using Antigravity and Claude Code. Expert-led guide for ambitious students.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' barrier is a ghost that haunts every junior developer. The solution is building a modular SaaS starter kit. By leveraging Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for rapid implementation, you transform abstract coding skills into a tangible, production-ready product that proves your engineering maturity to employers.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank repository, the silence of a missing portfolio echoing in your career prospects. Every job description demands 'proven experience,' yet the gatekeepers won't let you in without the very artifact you're struggling to create. The industry doesn't want another generic to-do list; they want to see how you handle state, authentication, and architectural scale.

Relief is found in the 'SaaS Starter Kit'—the Swiss Army knife of modern development. By building a reusable foundation for authentication, database integration, and UI patterns, you stop being a student and start being an architect. This isn't just code; it's your ticket to demonstrating the exact technical rigor that senior engineers look for in new hires.

steps.title: Define the Architectural Blueprint
steps.description: Establish the foundational stack. Focus on a robust T3-style architecture that handles scalability from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a high-level architectural overview for a SaaS starter kit including Next.js, Supabase, and Tailwind CSS. Focus on modularity and authentication flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'init project with Next.js, Supabase, and Tailwind, focusing on a modular folder structure'

steps.title: Implement Role-Based Access Control
steps.description: Authentication is the backbone of SaaS. Build a system that manages users, roles, and protected routes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure middleware implementation for role-based access control in a Next.js application using Supabase Auth.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'setup middleware for protected routes and define user roles in Supabase schema'

steps.title: Build the SaaS Subscription Logic
steps.description: Integrate Stripe to demonstrate your ability to handle external APIs and complex transaction lifecycles.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Outline the logic for a Stripe webhook handler to update user subscription status in a PostgreSQL database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create Stripe checkout session endpoint and webhook listener for subscription management'

steps.title: Design the Component Library
steps.description: Standardize your UI to show you understand design systems and maintainability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a set of reusable UI components including a dashboard layout, data tables, and input forms using Shadcn UI patterns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate reusable dashboard components using Tailwind and Radix UI primitives'

steps.title: Automate Deployment Pipelines
steps.description: CI/CD is non-negotiable. Set up automated testing and deployment to prove your production readiness.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Construct a GitHub Actions workflow for automated testing and deployment of a Next.js application to Vercel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure github actions workflow for continuous deployment to Vercel'

pro_tips: Always document your 'decision log' alongside your code; explain why you chose PostgreSQL over NoSQL to impress hiring managers.
Focus on 'Error Resilience'—write tests for the failure states, not just the happy path.
Implement an 'Audit Log' feature early; it shows you understand enterprise-grade data tracking requirements.
Use environment variables to separate configuration from code; never hardcode secrets, even in a portfolio project.
Prioritize accessibility (a11y) in your UI components; it separates amateur builds from professional-grade software.
content.next_steps: Deploy your kit to a live domain, add a 'Case Study' README file explaining your architectural choices, and share the repo on LinkedIn to attract potential employers.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical roadmap to build a portfolio piece that commands respect. Stop waiting for permission to be a developer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
